name: PLA Ground Force (PLAGF)
detail: ~915,000 active troops; world's largest army. Organized into 5 Theater Commands. Major modernization underway: replacing Soviet-era equipment with domestically developed Type 99A MBTs, PHL-03 rocket systems, and HQ-16/17 air defense systems.

name: PLA Navy (PLAN)
detail: World's largest navy by ship count: 355+ vessels including 3 aircraft carriers (Liaoning, Shandong, Fujian). Fujian (Type 003) is China's first catapult-equipped carrier. PLAN can project power from South China Sea to Indian Ocean. Rapid fleet expansion: 5-6 destroyers/frigates commissioned annually.

name: PLA Air Force (PLAAF)
detail: ~3,500 aircraft including J-20 stealth fighter (5th gen), J-16 multirole jets, and H-6K/N bombers. PLAAF has Asia's largest air arm. J-20 entered service 2017; second 5th-gen stealth fighter (J-35) in development for carrier operations.

name: PLA Rocket Force (PLARF)
detail: Controls China's nuclear and conventional ballistic/cruise missiles. DF-41 ICBM (range 12,000-15,000 km) capable of reaching all US cities. DF-21D/DF-26 'carrier killer' ASBMs specifically designed to hold US carrier groups at risk in Western Pacific. Undergoing major silo expansion (350+ new silos detected by CSIS).

name: Chinese Nuclear Arsenal
detail: ~500 warheads as of 2024 (SIPRI estimate); Pentagon projects 1,500 by 2035. Maintains 'minimum deterrence' posture with 'no-first-use' pledge, though US analysts question commitment. Land (DF-5B, DF-41), sea (JL-2/JL-3 on Type 094 SSBNs), and air (H-6N bomber) legs of triad all active.

name: PLA Strategic Support Force / Information Support Force
detail: Reorganized in 2024 into PLA Information Support Force, PLA Aerospace Force, and PLA Cyberspace Force. Operates BeiDou GPS constellation (35 satellites), Tiangong space station (crewed since 2021), and has demonstrated ASAT capabilities (2007 satellite destruction). PLA Unit 61398 linked to major US cyber intrusions.
